title: Stegnogramma subcalcarata
what_description: Stegnogramma subcalcarata is a species of fern in the family Aspleniaceae. It has a creeping rhizome with short and stout stipes, and lanceolate to ovate pinnae with 1-3 pairs of pinnules, which are obliquely rounded at the base and pinnatisect to nearly simple at the apex. The plant is dioecious, with sorus-bearing fronds having linear-lanceolate to lanceolate or ovate-lanceolate pinnules, while the sorus-bearing fronds are linear-lanceolate to lanceolate or ovate-lanceolate and have a linear-lanceolate to lanceolate or ovate-lanceolate pinnule at the base. The sporangia are 64-spored and borne on the underside of the pinnules. Stegnogramma subcalcarata is native to the Himalayas and southern China.

The plant's antimicrobial properties are also significant, with studies showing its ability to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, through the action of volatile oils and terpenoids. taxonomy: Stegnogramma subcalcarata is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the kingdom Plantae. It is further classified as a member of the phylum Pteridophyta, a group that includes all ferns and fern allies. Within the class Polypodiopsida, Stegnogramma subcalcarata is placed in the order Polypodiales, which is a large and diverse group of ferns. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary history and its relationships to other organisms in the plant kingdom.

intro: Stegnogramma subcalcarata, also known as Japanese Rattlesnake Grass or Rattlesnake Fern, is a type of fern plant native to East Asia. Traditionally, Stegnogramma subcalcarata is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to have therapeutic benefits in treating various health conditions such as arthritis, gout, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Stegnogramma subcalcarata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Stegnogramma subcalcarata is commonly prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its health benefits and alleviate symptoms associated with various diseases.
